meaning: adv.圆圆地,有力地,完全地,全面地
examples: The report has been roundly criticized.
For the Asian seat, Japan roundly defeated Iran.
Central bankers have roundly rejected the most obvious way to do that.
But his methods were roundly condemned by sectors of society that found themselves out of favour.
This is something that is already required by the current IPCC rules but roundly ignored.
